key_insight: The paper reframes structured preconditioning as a geometric certificate problem: how far a restricted metric family is from achieving a target condition-number class.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a principled way to judge whether diagonal, block, Kronecker, or low-rank preconditioners are feasible before investing in solver tuning.
method: Defines restricted dynamic geometric complexity as an intrinsic distance-to-reachability measure under metric-family constraints.
Proves monotonicity and submanifold-distance principles, then casts diagonal/block reachability as LMI feasibility.
Derives exact or computable certificates for 2D diagonal cases, Kronecker projections, and Hessian-relative candidate tests.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports deterministic toy and synthetic workflows that verify diagonal, block, and Kronecker certificate behavior on small quadratic instances.
watch_item: Several results are diagnostic or certificate-based rather than full optimizer characterizations, so practical use depends on how well the chosen metric family matches the real problem structure.
paper_title: Restricted Dynamic Geometric Complexity: Certificates for Structured Preconditioning
